name: agent-skills-marketplace
description: A curated collection of Claude Code skills and plugins for developers

plugins.name: daily-carry
plugins.description: Essential daily-use skills: Portainer deployment management and Tech Research Enforcer for verified coding answers

plugins.name: release-automation
plugins.description: Automated release workflow for any project type with version bumping, changelog generation, documentation sync, and git operations. Supports Node.js, Python, Rust, Go, Java, generic projects, monorepos, and Claude Code plugins.

plugins.name: diataxis
plugins.description: Documentation writing and review skills based on the Diátaxis framework. Seven skills covering all four documentation quadrants (tutorial, how-to, reference, explanation), plus README landing pages, a doc planner, and a docs reviewer.

plugins.name: sdlc-workflow
plugins.description: Complete SDLC workflow with integrated code review, unified design system, Diátaxis documentation, analysis, observability, and git lifecycle. 32 lifecycle commands including wf-how, wf-ideate, 8 standalone workflows (wf-hotfix, wf-update-deps, wf-docs, wf-refactor, wf-quick, wf-rca, wf-investigate, wf-discover), 2 augmentations (wf-instrument, wf-experiment), wf-benchmark, wf-profile, wf-validate pre-write hook, 31 review commands, 7 aggregate reviews, 15 skills, and setup-wide-logging. v8.25: wf-skip (skip a stage without running it). v8.26: wf-close (archive a workflow at any stage; five close reasons). v8.27: Unified wf-design — 14 standalone design-* skills and 5 wf-design* commands collapsed into a single wf-design command with 22 sub-commands. New imagegen skill (built-in image_gen → gpt-image-2 → nano-banana-pro → text fallback). Imagegen probes in shape and craft. v8.28: SDLC-aware wf-design routing. craft splits (workflow → 02c-craft.md visual contract; freestanding → writes code). 15 transformations register as augmentations. v8.29: Closed artifact-consumption gap. Post-design stages (slice, plan, implement, verify, review, handoff, ship, retro) now consume design artifacts and every augmentation type (instrument, experiment, benchmark, design-*) with type-specific behavior: implement adds instrumentation signals + experiment wiring as it codes; verify re-runs per-type checks (axe-core, perf, cohort, compare-mode); handoff/ship translate augmentations to user-language changelog entries. Visual contract enforced via new § Visual Contract Honored (in implement record) and § Augmentation Verification (in verify record). Workflow-type awareness added: compressed mode (wf-quick → 01-quick.md) and forwarded mode (wf-rca/wf-investigate → 01-rca.md as rich context). Fixes a critical broken contract where wf-quick produced 01-quick.md but wf-implement only read 02-shape.md. v8.30: Per-slice review artifacts. wf-review now writes 07-review-<slice>.md and 07-review-<slice>-<command>.md so running review on a second slice no longer overwrites the first. wf-handoff parses every per-slice review's verdict + blocker count from frontmatter and refuses to proceed if any slice has unresolved blockers. Consumers updated: wf-implement reviews mode, wf-amend/wf-extend from-review, wf-retro, wf-ship, wf-skip, wf-status, wf-how findings. v8.30.1: wf-implement now loads design references named in 02b-design.md's recommended-references frontmatter array (read-only rationale during implementation). reference/shape.md typo fixed (typography.md → typeset.md) and frontmatter mirror mandated. v8.31.0: No-optional-artifacts policy across the lifecycle. Every stage's

across wf-slice, wf-plan, wf-implement, wf-verify, wf-review, wf-handoff, wf-ship, wf-retro. New uniform rule: if any conditional input file exists on disk, the stage MUST read it and the stage's output MUST honor it. Existence is optional; consumption is required. Per-row text strengthened with explicit MUST language (e.g., implement MUST honor 02b register/anti-goals; verify MUST re-verify 02c mock fidelity inventory; ship MUST add a changelog entry per augmentation). wf-slice now consumes 02c-craft.md (was missing entirely) plus binding consumption of 02b-design.md: distinct states/surfaces MUST become slice boundaries, or 03-slice.md ## Slice Strategy MUST justify any grouping. Closes the broader gap where present artifacts could be silently ignored under the prior 'optional' framing.
